stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says Russia unlikely to accept – ‘for them, nothing is sacred’; Australian police arrest army reservist for joining war. What we know on day 1,504 Ukraine’s president has renewed his offer to Russia of a mutual ceasefire on strikes against energy infrastructure . “If Russia is ready to stop strikes on our energy infrastructure, we will respond in kind,” he said. “This proposal has been conveyed to the Russian side through the Americans.” Volodymyr Zelenskyy offered last week

stories.title: Hungary pipeline false-flag claims swirl as JD Vance makes election intervention
stories.summary: Claims explosives found near pipeline come before election in which PM Viktor Orbán is trailing in most polls Hungary has placed the gas pipeline that straddles the Serbian border under military protection, the prime minister, Viktor Orbán, has said, as accusations of a false-flag operation continued to swirl before a crunch election at the weekend and an official visit on Tuesday from the US vice-president, JD Vance. Orbán travelled to Hungary’s southern border with Serbia on Monday, one day af

stories.title: JD Vance on standby in US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance is on standby, prepared to jump into sensitive negotiations with Iran if backchannel talks advance to the point of a direct meeting with Iranian officials. The talks, currently, are led by Steve Witkoff, the president’s special envoy, and Jared Kushner, the president’s son-in-law, but Vance could be tagged in if the pair make sufficient progress, according to a person familiar with the talks granted anonymity to discuss them. It is not clear what Iran might need to offer.

stories.title: From ‘stink bugs’ to ‘enemies of the people’: how Viktor Orbán blazed a trail for Trump’s media assaults
stories.summary: Hungary’s prime minister has conducted a systematic attack on independent media. The parallels with the US are chilling During his state of the nation address earlier this year, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, outlined a chilling vision of the country’s future. Signalling a new level of aggression in his campaign against the truth if he is returned to power in elections on 12 April, Orbán vowed to purge the country of “bought journalists” and “fake civil society organisations”. Media rep

stories.title: Tuesday briefing: What is driving the record rise in stalking offences?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As cases charged hit their highest level on record in England and Wales, a look at what is behind the increase and whether the criminal justice system is keeping pace Good morning. Disturbing new data shows that stalking offences recorded by police in England and Wales have surged over the past decade, with sharp rises in every region. The number of cases charged by the Crown Prosecution Service has also climbed to the highest level on record. But those figures only tell p

stories.title: EU foreign policy shambles triggers calls for radical overhaul of diplomacy
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Europe’s mounting foreign policy failures, from struggling to fund Ukraine to its fragmented response to the Iran war, are fueling calls for a root-and-branch overhaul of how the bloc conducts diplomacy. The EU’s inability to take unified decisions — such as unblocking a €90 billion loan for Kyiv , imposing sanctions on violent West Bank settlers and implementing measures targeting Russia — exposes a systemic paralysis, nine EU diplomats, officials, lawmakers and experts to

stories.title: Serbia looks for pipeline sabotage plot culprit as Vance's Hungary visit nears
stories.summary: Serbia has opened a probe into the attempted pipeline sabotage after explosives were found near Kanjiža, as military intelligence suspects a migrant with training, raising the temperature before a key election in Hungary on Sunday.

stories.title: Second ex-staffer accuses Texas lawmaker of sending sexually explicit messages
stories.summary: Republican Tony Gonzales ended re-election bid in March after admitting to having affair with a different aide Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A second former female staffer for Tony Gonzales, a Republican congressman from Texas , has come forward claiming Gonzales sent her sexually explicit messages. The San Antonio Express-News first reported the text messages on Monday and NBC News later confirmed the report. Continue reading...

stories.title: JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
stories.summary: The US vice-president's visit is the latest show of White House support for the Hungarian leader.

stories.title: Germany looks to soften controversial military registration law
stories.summary: BERLIN — Germany is scrambling to clarify a controversial rule requiring that men aged 17 to 45 seek permission before leaving the country for more than three months, after the measure triggered a political backlash over the weekend. The German Defense Ministry, which wrote the legislation late last year, told POLITICO it is now considering exemptions to the requirement, as officials seek to limit its practical impact even as the law remains in force. “The Defense Ministry is currently developin

stories.title: Artemis II swings back around after completing record-setting moon flyby
stories.summary: Astronauts become Earth’s farthest travelled and exceed a 1970 record on the fifth day of the mission Blackouts, broken records and a message from the past: five key moments from Artemis II’s lunar flyby Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Artemis II astronauts broke Apollo 13’s distance record at 1.57pm eastern time on Monday, hugging each other in the cramped capsule as they made history for becoming the first four humans to travel the farthest from Ea

stories.title: House Democrats demand end to ‘cruel’ US energy blockade after visit to Cuba
stories.summary: Pramila Jayapal and Jonathan Jackson denounce ‘collective punishment’ amid vast disruption s from US oil blockade Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Two Democratic US lawmakers on Monday called for an end to the “cruel collective punishment” of Cuba after they visited the island to witness the effects of an US energy blockade. The US House members Pramila Jayapal of Washington and Jonathan Jackson of Illinois met with the Cuban president, Miguel Díaz-Ca

stories.title: Hungarian Easter tradition sees women doused with water in Hollókő
stories.summary: A centuries-old Easter tradition continues in a Hungarian village, where men pour water over women in a ritual rooted in folklore and springtime customs.

stories.title: Mexican art world protests over plan to send Frida Kahlo masterpieces to Spain
stories.summary: Cultural figures sign open letter asking government for clarity on how long landmark collection will remain abroad One of the world’s most important collections of 20th-century Mexican art, including works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, is set to be exported to Spain under an agreement with Banco Santander , sparking outrage among Mexico’s cultural community. Nearly 400 cultural professionals have signed an open letter calling on the Mexican government to offer greater clarity on what the deal

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: Warsaw’s Neon Museum sparks revival of interest in cold war signs and aesthetic
stories.summary: After fall of communism, signs were left to rust until museum founders began to collect and restore them As they struggled through the decades of cold war gloom and repression, Warsaw’s neon signs became symbols of light, colour and hope of brighter days. What had started as a form of Soviet propaganda sparked a wave of creativity in the Polish capital that even the Communist authorities could not crush. But after communism ended in the late 1980s, many of the signs lost their purpose and began

stories.title: Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatal, személyes felelősség, a hatalomátvétel folyamata – a Magyar Péterrel készült interjú legfontosabb pillanatai videón
stories.summary: Hogy nézne ki a hatalomátvétel? Mi lenne a válasza a migrációs kvótára? Hogyan békélne meg a Fidesszel? A Magyar Péterrel készült, közel másfél órás élő interjúnk legfontosabb pillanatai.

stories.title: Magyar Péter azt ígéri, hogy vasárnap ott lesznek minden szavazókörnél, és mindent rögzíteni fognak
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke szerint Orbán Viktor újra elővette a félelemkártyát a Török Áramlat elleni állítólagos merényletkísérlettel, és harmadosztályú operett-előadásnak nevezte a látottakat.

stories.title: Rendkívüli lehetőség, ne hagyd ki! – ezzel toborozták a DPK-sokat J.D. Vance keddi látogatására
stories.summary: Hétfő délután a Fidesz még nagy erőkkel kereste a résztvevőket, azóta a regisztrációs limit betelt.

stories.title: A magyarok fele szerint a Fidesz elcsalhatja a választást, többen tartanak orosz, mint ukrán beavatkozástól
stories.summary: A Political Capital felkérésére készült kutatás szerint többen számítanak a Tisza győzelmére, de a kormánypárti választók a magabiztosabbak. A közvélemény egyre kevésbé vevő a Fidesz politikai mítoszaira.

stories.title: Elég baj, de a panellakótelepek mutatnak irányt az új lakóparkoknak
stories.summary: Minden érintett tudja, milyen irányba kellene lépni a budapesti lakásépítésekkel, mégsem haladunk arra.

stories.title: Az álmennyezet felett halmokban állt a fekete por – itt vannak a Samsungból kiszivárgott legdurvább fotók és dokumentumok
stories.summary: Súlyos technikai problémák, igénytelenség és a rendkívül laza hatósági kontroll vezetett ahhoz, hogy a gödi Samsung sok dolgozója éveken keresztül rákkeltő anyagokkal teli levegőben végezte a munkáját. Ebben a cikkben bemutatjuk azokat a jegyzőkönyveket, amelyek szerint a hatósági dokumentumoknál is súlyosabb mérgezések történtek a gyárban.

stories.title: Elemző: Szakadék szélén áll a Fidesz, és az RMDSZ is ott van mellette
stories.summary: A Fidesz rendszerének megingása nemcsak Magyarországon, hanem Erdélyben is érezteti a hatását. Kiss Tamás szociológus szerint az RMDSZ olyan helyzetbe sodródott, ahol már nincs igazán jó választás.

stories.title: Orbán Magyar Péternél egy órával később zárja a kampányt szombaton
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök Budapesten, a Tisza Párt elnöke Debrecenben mond beszédet szombat este.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor elengedte a kormányt
stories.summary: De nem azt.

stories.title: Átjelentkezések: a XI., a XIII. és a XIV. kerületnek kell a leginkább felkészülnie Budapesten
stories.summary: Csütörtökön 16 óráig lehetett jelezni, ha valaki nem a lakóhelye szerinti szavazókörben, hanem egy másik magyarországi településen szeretne szavazni.

stories.title: A kisbériek megnyerték háborújukat a szarszag ellen
stories.summary: Összefogásukkal pedig megmutatták, hogyan lehet fellépni a környezetszennyező cégek ellen. Évek kellettek hozzá, de végül térdre kényszerítették a vállalkozót, akinek a biogázüzeme kibírhatatlan bűzzel árasztja el a várost.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor levelével együtt érkezik a napelemtárolási program eredménye a nyerteseknek
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök propagandalevélben mond köszönetet a nyerteseknek, amiben persze előkerül a szuverenitás és a rezsicsökkentés is.

stories.title: A bántalmazás miatt szerveznék át a javítókat, miközben a börtönökben még rosszabb a helyzet
stories.summary: Ellentétes a gyerekek érdekeivel, ha a büntetés-végrehajtás befolyását erősítik a javítóintézetek helyett. A Magyar Helsinki Bizottság szerint a Szőlő utcai ügy után a javítóintézetek fejlesztésére lenne szükség, nem a börtönstruktúra erősítésére.

stories.title: Úgy akarunk kijutni a foci-vb-re, hogy a korosztályos válogatottjaink súlytalanok
stories.summary: A vb-résztvevő Csehország 15 év alatt 14-szer játszott utánpótlás-Eb-n, a magyar válogatott ötször, ebből háromszor rendező volt. Ausztria vb-döntős volt az U17-ben, de Bosznia is sikeresebb az erősen megtámogatott magyar rendszernél. Csányi Sándor MLSZ-elnök is illúziókban él, pedig a foci tanulható részében maradunk le.

stories.title: Erre ne autózzon Budapesten, ha nem akar dugóban állni az amerikai alelnök fuvarozása miatt
stories.summary: Térképen mutatjuk merre azt a kismillió helyet a fővárosban, ahova ne is parkoljon a következő két napban, ha nem akar napokig koslatni az elszállított autója után.

stories.title: Marco Rossi úgy látja, kiváló munkát végzett, a második legjobb csapat volt az övé, bár a tabella mást mutat
stories.summary: A magyar kapitány most is megerősítette, Olaszországból még soha nem kapott ajánlatot.

stories.title: 2,5 millió forintért cserélnének motort Péter Peugeot-jában, pedig elvileg ingyen kellene
stories.summary: A mindössze 130 ezer kilométert futott családi autó motorja ezer kilométerenként több mint egy liter olajat fogyaszt. A probléma Magyarországon is rengeteg Citroën, Opel és Peugeot gazdáját érinti.

stories.title: Animációban mutatják meg, mi nincs rendben a levélszavazással Erdélyben
stories.summary: A legnagyobb romániai civil szervezet videója azt mutatja meg, hogyan kerül a szavazat a választótól egy többlépcsős, nehezen követhető rendszerbe, mielőtt eljutna a választási irodához.

stories.title: Én leszek az, aki az ellenzék jogosítványait védeni fogja – interjú Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: Nagyon gyorsan feláll és rengeteg dolga lesz a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatalnak, és azért van szükség lehetőleg kétharmadra, hogy a Fidesz kevésbé tudjon mókolni – élőben interjúztunk Magyar Péterrel.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor a Politicónak: Nem engem kéne lecsukni, hanem Orbán Viktort és a bandáját
stories.summary: A lelkész azt mondta a lapnak, hálás volt, amikor 1989-ben egy hosszú hajú fiatalember hazaküldte az oroszokat, és sosem gondolta, hogy majd ő fogja visszahívni őket.

stories.title: Poland's border with Belarus sees decrease in illegal immigration attempts by 96%
stories.summary: The Polish government has announced a significant decrease in the number of attempts to illegally cross the border with Belarus, pointing to the effectiveness of its migration policy and the reinforced activities of its services.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: German XXL frigate on the horizon - but when will the super warship arrive?
stories.summary: Germany does not have an aircraft carrier, but is planning the largest frigate in the world - the F126. It is due to set sail in 2029 and will be suitable for submarine hunting or drone operations, depending on the mission. But there are problems with the vessel. When will the super ship arrive?

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: Resident doctors’ strike has torpedoed pay rises and training posts, says Wes Streeting
stories.summary: Exclusive: Health secretary says industrial action in England also threatens to derail NHS progress on waiting times Wes Streeting has accused resident doctors of “torpedoing” their own pay rises and training jobs by walking out on strike again, as tens of thousands of doctors began a six-day stoppage in England. stories.title: Pentagon’s new plans in Iran give Trump a way out of war crime accusations
stories.summary: The Pentagon is expanding a list of Iranian energy sites it can target for attacks to include ones that provide fuel and power to both civilians and the military, a likely workaround if the administration is accused of war crimes for striking basic infrastructure. War planners are revising the list, according to two defense officials, as American and Israeli warplanes search for new targets after five weeks of around-the-clock strikes on military sites and U.S. ground troops surge into the regio

stories.title: Trump says he would ‘take the oil’ in Iran — except that Americans want the war to end
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday said he would like to “take” Iran’s oil — but that he hasn’t because Americans want to see the war brought to a close. “If I had my choice, I’d keep the oil. But I also want to make the people of our country happy,” Trump told reporters at the annual White House Easter egg roll. “Unfortunately the American people would like to see us come home. stories.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist who reported on crew’s rescue in Iran if they don’t reveal source
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday threatened to find and jail the journalist who first reported that a U.S. troop was awaiting rescue after his plane was shot down in Iran last week, unless that journalist revealed their source. Speaking from the White House, Trump said a “leak” told an unidentified reporter that while  one pilot had been rescued  shortly after an F-15 fighter jet crashed in Iran on Friday, a second officer had not yet been found. stories.summary: With the UAE under fire from Iranian missiles, wealthy investors are turning to Italy’s flat-tax haven Just over a month ago, Dubai was the obvious destination for wealthy Britons in search of a new home. Few cities allow you to earn vast sums tax-free and spend them across any number of luxury hotels, restaurants and shops. Banks, insurers and investment managers said their businesses were growing, with a positive balance of nearly two-thirds noting an expansion, according to a long-running survey by the Confederation of British Industry (CBI)

stories.title: Congo joins US list of third-country deportation destinations
stories.summary: The DRC said it would "temporarily" receive migrants from the US, becoming the latest African nation to agree to do so.

stories.title: Ukraine: 3 killed in Odesa, Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
stories.summary: Russian drone strikes on the southern Ukrainian city of Odesa left at least three people dead overnight, including a 2-year-old girl. Meanwhile, Ukraine has continued retaliatory strikes against Russian oil facilities.

stories.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
stories.summary: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.

stories.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes first China visit since 2016
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun said she "gladly accepted" Xi Jinping's invitation and hopes to be a "bridge for peace".

stories.title: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal with US as war of words deepens
stories.summary: Iran rejects a new 45-day ceasefire proposal, while the US is still expected to respond, as US President Donald Trump delays a threatened military strike tied to the Strait of Hormuz. Mediators are pushing for talks, but tensions remain high on both sides.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.summary: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump: ‘I can’t tell you’ whether war is winding down or escalating
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday said he couldn’t know if the war with Iran was ending , or escalating, ahead of his Tuesday 8 p.m. EDT deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. “I can’t tell you, I don’t know. I can’t tell you. Depends what they do,” Trump said when asked whether he is winding down the war, as he has previously suggested, or ramping up, referring to his threat to bomb bridges and power plants. “This is a critical period. They have a period of, well, until tomorrow at 8

stories.title: California county records sixth person bitten by rattlesnake in under a month
stories.summary: Two fatalities reported in southern California so far, with warmer spring bringing reptiles out on trails earlier A sixth person has been bitten by a rattlesnake in southern California’s Ventura county in just under a month, two-thirds of the number of people bitten in all of 2025. Andrew Dowd, a Ventura county fire department spokesperson, said paramedics responded to a call on Sunday for a man who had been bitten by a rattlesnake. stories.title: Down the rabbit hole: Trump offers dark Iran warnings after Easter bunny act

stories.title: Judge says Lil Nas X police battery charges to be dismissed if he completes treatment program
stories.summary: Rapper ‘very thankful’ to be given chance to enter mental health diversion program after arrest in LA last year A judge has allowed Lil Nas X to enter a mental health diversion program intended to lead to the dismissal of charges of attacking Los Angeles police officers. Judge Alan Schneider told the rapper and singer on Monday that if he sticks to his treatment program and obeys all laws for two years, his four felony counts will be dismissed. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns of ‘devastating’ retaliation after Trump’s expletive-laden threats over strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – our live coverage continues here A Japanese shipping firm said on Monday that an Indian-flagged tanker owned by its subsidiary had passed through the strait of Hormuz and was en route to India. A spokeswoman for Mitsui O.S.K. Lines told AFP that the Green Asha – a liquefied petroleum gas tanker – had crossed the waterway. Pakistan stands in solidarity with the brotherly people of the UAE and reiterates the urgent need for restraint and de-escalation in the region. Conti

stories.title: Spanish politicians clash over request to move Picasso’s Guernica
stories.summary: Madrid and Basque government leaders call each other ‘provincial’ in dispute over the artwork A row has broken out between the Madrid and Basque regional governments in Spain over the latter’s request for Guernica, probably Picasso’s most celebrated work, to be housed temporarily in the Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao to mark the 90th anniversary of the bombing of the Basque town. The work has hung in the Reina Sofía museum in Madrid since 1992 and repeated requests for it to be moved to the Basque

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reform UK would stop visas for people from countries seeking slavery reparations
stories.summary: The ‘bank is closed and the door is locked’, says Zia Yusuf as calls grow for compensation to remedy historical wrongs Reform UK has said it would stop issuing visas to any person from a country which continues to demand compensation from the UK for its role in the transatlanctic trade in enslaved people. Zia Yusuf, the party’s home affairs spokesperson, told the Daily Telegraph that the call for reparations was “insulting”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Thailand PM urges working from home amid fears over energy crisis
stories.summary: Anutin Charnvirakul encourages measures such as home working and carpooling as country is reliant on oil imports Thailand’s prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, has called on the public to conserve energy, urging work-from-home measures and carpooling, as he warned of the impact of the conflict in the Middle East. In a statement posted on social media, Anutin said Thailand was exposed to the crisis because of its reliance on imported oil and gas, and the country could not be complacent. Continue

stories.title: Greek PM vows to tackle ‘deep state’ in wake of farm fraud scandal
stories.summary: Kyriakos Mitsotakis calls alleged scamming of EU agricultural funds ‘a turning point’ The Greek prime minister has vowed to tackle what he has called a “deep state” he says is plaguing the country, as he sought to address a growing political crisis over a farm fraud scandal that has forced the resignation of multiple government ministers. In a speech, aired on national TV, Kyriakos Mitsotakis attempted to limit the damage, describing the revelations as “a turning point” that had turbo-charged hi

stories.title: Artemis II breaks record for farthest human spaceflight
stories.summary: The Orion spacecraft is in its final phase, with NASA saying astronauts have broken Apollo 13's record for traveling the farthest distance from Earth.

stories.title: Vietnam elects Communist Party leader To Lam as president
stories.summary: The election of To Lam as the state president marks a shift in Vietnam's power structure where party leadership and state presidency were held by different people.

stories.title: Australia arrests decorated veteran Ben Roberts-Smith on war crimes charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith is a Victoria Cross recipient and had been linked to the murder of unarmed civilians while deployed in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Can small modular nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular nuclear reactors are essential to meeting energy and climate goals, but critics slam them as a "costly distraction" that "won't solve any of our energy problems."

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader heads to China for 'peace'
stories.summary: Kuomintang's chairwoman Cheng Li-wun hopes to meet Xi Jinping during her visit to China. Her goal, she said, is to "to preserve peace is to preserve Taiwan."

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran defiant as Trump deadline nears
stories.summary: Tehran says it wants a permanent end to the war but refused to fully open the Strait of Hormuz, despite President Donald Trump warning it to meet his demands or be "taken out." Follow DW for more.

stories.title: North Korea welcomes Seoul regret over drones as 'wise'
stories.summary: Pyongyang leader Kim Jong Un's sister says it was "very fortunate and wise" for South Korea's president to express regret for drone incursions into the North. Seoul had initially denied it was behind the survey flight.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.summary: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.summary: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Easter festivities muted for Syrian Christians after recent violence
stories.summary: Syrian Christians saw their Easter celebrations confined to church prayers after armed groups attacked the town of Al-Suqaylabiyah in Hama on 27 March. Euronews spoke to the faithful in the country on one of Christianity's most important holidays.

stories.title: Locals dispute Nigerian army claim of 31 rescued after Easter attack
stories.summary: Five civilians were killed by the gunmen and their bodies were recovered at the scene, the army said.

stories.title: UAE tells Euronews ceasefire not enough for Iran war solution, says 'no trust' in Tehran regime
stories.summary: In a briefing to Euronews, the UAE president's diplomatic advisor called for a durable regional security solution beyond a ceasefire with the US as central to the Gulf’s security, as “there is no trust” in the current Iranian regime, but also sees a “more prominent Israeli influence in the Gulf.”

stories.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
stories.summary: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Warm March in US leaves snowpack critically low
stories.summary: Concerns about coming wildfire risk, and temperatures also remain high on other side of Pacific where rare tropical cyclone has formed After a historically warm winter across nine states in the US, the first month of meteorological spring again brought exceptionally high temperatures, with numerous states recording new all-time high temperatures in March. The remarkable intensity and longevity of the warmth have left much of the mountain snowpack, a crucial source of water for millions in the Am

stories.title: ‘Such a mix of people’: Ireland of 1926 was not monocultural, release of census shows
stories.summary: Archive is freely available online from 18 April, revealing the lives, occupations and secrets of 2.9m people The first years of independent Ireland tend to be remembered, if at all, as a dreary monochrome of parochialism and conservatism. After the blazing dramas of the 1916 rebellion and the 1919-1921 Anglo-Irish war , the infant state seemed to limp into a grey period of insularity, the dream of freedom giving way to anti-climax and drab conformity. Continue reading...

stories.title: German mayors call for night-time ban on robot lawnmowers to protect hedgehogs
stories.summary: Leaders say automated mowers’ blades threaten nocturnal animals as studies highlight risks to wildlife German mayors have called for a nationwide ban on night-time use of robot lawnmowers to protect hedgehogs and other small nocturnal animals from being killed or maimed in the dark. Recent studies have highlighted the threat lawnmower blades pose to wildlife active between dusk and dawn, prompting growing calls for regulation. Hedgehogs also tend to curl into a ball when threatened rather than r

stories.title: Dozens of firms risk losing B Corp status after standards overhaul
stories.summary: Tougher ethical certification process requires companies to meet standards in every one out of seven categories Dozens of companies may be at risk of losing their coveted B Corp ethical status after the organisation behind the corporate kite-marking system raised the standards required to qualify. B Lab, which oversees B Corp certification, launched the biggest overhaul in its 19-year history earlier this month, scrapping a system under which companies must gather enough points across multiple c

stories.title: India news: PM Modi addresses election rallies in Assam
stories.summary: An election race is in full swing in Assam as it goes to polls this week. Prime Minister Narendra Modi is attending three campaign rallies in the northeastern state on Monday.

stories.title: Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives
stories.summary: As the US and Israel continue their war with Iran, airstrikes on Iranian pharmaceutical and medical facilities are harming thousands of people by denying them medical treatment, doctors and activists warn.

stories.title: Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?
stories.summary: The head of the Myanmar's junta Min Aung Hlaing has swapped his uniform for the top civilian office, but the move is unlikely to bring peace or even create distance from the authoritarian system imposed by the 2021 coup.

stories.title: North Korea: Kim's daughter now seen as likely heir — South
stories.summary: Seoul's spy service says the North Korean leader's "most beloved" daughter is increasingly seen as a likely successor. Officials say her appearances at military events are meant to normalize the idea of a female heir.

stories.title: OPEC+ to hike crude output: Will it make a difference to oil prices?
stories.summary: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) has agreed ‌to increase its crude output as the Middle East conflict continues to disrupt oil traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Spanish police find historical manuscript missing for a century for sale online
stories.summary: Spanish police recovered a 17th-century velvet guild manuscript that had been missing for a century after spotting it for sale online. The artefact will remain the property of its current owner, but it will now be conserved.

stories.title: In Portugal, bikers join Father Avelino for Easter blessing
stories.summary: In Sintra, Father Avelino leads an Easter motorbike parade, taking the traditional compasso blessing to the streets and uniting bikers in faith.

stories.title: Israel strikes Iran's largest petrochemical facility as Trump's Hormuz deadline looms
stories.summary: Earlier on Monday, Iran warned of more devastating retaliation if the US hits civilian targets, as mediators pushed a 45-day ceasefire to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Revellers parade giant phalluses at Japan’s fertility festival
stories.summary: Crowds pack Kawasaki for Kanamara Matsuri, Japan’s colourful fertility festival using phallic shrines to celebrate life and challenge sex taboos.

stories.title: Going abroad: What will Germany's new military service act actually change
stories.summary: Under the new law, German men aged 17 to 45 must obtain authorisation for extended stays abroad. The obligation is not new, but critics say it should have been communicated more clearly.

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security in unexpected military alliance
stories.summary: Ukraine's Zelenskyy arrived in Damascus in a surprise visit on Sunday, for his first official visit to Syria after the fall of the pro-Russian al-Assad regime.

stories.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
stories.summary: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

stories.title: Mitsotakis urges swift EU probe into MPs over Greek farm subsidy fraud
stories.summary: Greek PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis has asked EU prosecutors to swiftly charge MPs over alleged €23m farm subsidy fraud, as resignations and pressure mount.

stories.title: Young gray whale dies after swimming up river in Washington state
stories.summary: Hunger is a likely reason the whale, dubbed Willapa Willy, travelled inland, experts say.

stories.title: Nasa’s Artemis II crew to reach unseen far side of the Moon on flyby
stories.summary: NASA’s Artemis II crew are expected to reach their destination on Monday where the four astronauts aboard the Orion will – for the first time – look at the lunar far side with the naked eye. The fly-by will last approximately six hours before the astronauts head back home.

stories.title: DR Congo agrees to take deportees from the US
stories.summary: The country will start receiving deportees from this month but has not said how many they will accept.

stories.title: How can Ukraine help unlock the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened to unleash "hell" on Iran if it failed to meet his Tuesday deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping. Can Ukraine help unblock it?

stories.title: US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction
stories.summary: The order allows a lower court to consider dismissing the former Trump strategist's indictment.

stories.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
stories.summary: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.

stories.title: 'Really feeling the love' - Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC as search for mother goes on
stories.summary: Nancy Guthrie disappeared from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in what authorities believe was an abduction.

stories.title: Trump warns Iran it could be destroyed 'in one night' as deadline nears
stories.summary: Donald Trump has once again escalated threats against Iran, warning the country could be “taken out in one night” as the US deadline for Tehran approaches. Iran has rejected a proposed ceasefire, insisting on a permanent end to the war, while fighting continues to intensify across the region.

stories.title: Trump endorses ex-UK political aide Steve Hilton for California governor
stories.summary: Steve Hilton, who advised the former prime minster and hosted a Fox News show, is running as a Republican.

stories.title: A GVH vezető közgazdásza: Van a NER-es rész, ahol nem nagyon tudunk mit tenni
stories.summary: Berezvai Zombor egy, a Partizánnak adott interjúban beszélt a hivatal és a politika összefonódásáról. A GVH ezt tagadja, válaszukban azt írták, a hivatal szakmai alapon, befolyásmentesen működik.

stories.title: Évi 150 millió forint egy szoftvermérnöknek, 115 millió egy termékmenedzsernek – kiderült, hogy fizet a Meta
stories.summary: Nemcsak forintba átszámolva, hanem dollárban is kiemelkedően jól keresnek a külföldről importált szakemberek, még a belépő szint is jóval magasabb a kaliforniai átlagbérnél.

stories.title: Ha mindig is hamburgerológus akart lenni, tárt karokkal várja a McDonald's saját egyeteme
stories.summary: Már ha be tud jutni, mert az nem lesz egyszerű. A Hamburger Egyetemnek szerte a világon vannak campusai, a képzésnek pedig abszolút van értelme.

stories.title: Trump eltörölt több, a transznemű diákok egyenlő oktatási esélyeiről szóló megállapodást
stories.summary: A Trump-kormányzat eddig legalább 40 polgárjogi vizsgálatot indított olyan oktatási intézmények ellen, amik védelmet biztosítanak transznemű diákoknak.

stories.title: Meglőtték Offset reppert Floridában
stories.summary: A rendőrség szerint nem szenvedett életveszélyes sérüléseket.

stories.title: Gy. Németh a volt szocialista politikusok felszólítására: Lehet nyomást gyakorolni. De nem lépünk vissza!
stories.summary: A DK országgyűlési képviselője szerint „könnyű okoskodni tévéstúdiókból meg tanácsadói szerződések mögül”, de ők nem egy újabb egypárti uralomért dolgoznak.

stories.title: Trump börtönnel fenyeget újságírókat, ha nem adják ki iráni forrásaikat
stories.summary: Az ENSZ Biztonsági Tanácsa szavazhat ma a Hormuzi-szoros helyzetéről. Hírfolyamunk a közel-keleti háborúról.

stories.title: A debreceni tiszás jelölt posztolt egy hangfelvételt, amin szerinte a fideszes alpolgármester beszél arról, hogyan segíti a Jobbik jelöltjét
stories.summary: Tárkányi Zsolt azt állítja, Barcsa Lajos Aba-Horváth Istvánnal beszélget a felvételen arról, hogy tolják a Jobbik szekerét, mert szavazatokat vesz el a Tiszától.

stories.title: Marad a napos, szeles idő, csütörtöktől visszatérnek a hajnali fagyok
stories.summary: Kedden 20 fok is lehet, szerdától hűvösebb idő jön.

stories.title: Berendelt a honvédelmi miniszter, és három ajánlatot tett – újra megszólalt Pálinkás Szilveszter százados
stories.summary: A százados ismét kamera elé állt, hogy elmesélje, hogy mivel próbálta őt lekenyerezni Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf miniszter, hogy ne adjon interjút, de nem tartja magát megvásárolhatónak. Interjú Pálinkás Szilveszterrel az elmúlt napokról.

stories.title: Pálinkás Szilveszter elmondta, hogyan próbálták rávenni, hogy ne álljon a nyilvánosság elé
stories.summary: Berendelte az irodájába Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf, és három ajánlatot tett le az asztalra. A százados azt érezte, hogy a miniszter attól fél, hogy olyanok derülhetnek ki róla, amik rossz fényt vetnének rá és a minisztériumra. Pálinkás elmesélte, hogy mi történt vele az első interjú megjelenése óta, és milyen visszajelzéseket kapott a katonáktól.

stories.title: Az Európai Bizottság arra figyelmezteti a tagállamokat, hogy ne adósodjanak el túlságosan az energiaválság miatt
stories.summary: Több ország is beavatkozott a benzin- és dízelárak alakulásába, az EB szerint ezt csak korlátosan szabadna megtenni.

stories.title: RFI Investigation: Ukrainian military in Libya and the strike on the Russian gas carrier Arctic Metagaz
stories.summary: French journalists report the presence of more than 200 Ukrainian military specialists in Libya and their alleged involvement in the attack on the Arctic Metagaz vessel using a maritime drone.

stories.title: Meloni in the Gulf: harmony on gas and defence with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The focus of the talks of Italian prime minister with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia was the restoration of energy supplies and security in Hormuz. Italy offered technical support for affected infrastructure and defence against Iranian raids.

stories.title: Cubans study oil tanker diplomacy for signs of progress in secret talks with US
stories.summary: Despite hostile rhetoric Trump let a Russian ship break his blockade – could it herald a Venezuela-style outcome? When a sanctioned Russian oil tanker, the Anatoly Kolodkin, docked at Cuba’s Matanzas oil terminal on Tuesday, unloading 700,000 barrels of crude , it was not immediately clear why the ship had been allowed to pass through Donald Trump’s oil blockade. In January, the US president had proclaimed on social media: “THERE WILL BE NO MORE OIL OR MONEY GOING TO CUBA – ZERO!” yet last week

stories.title: US pilot rescued as Trump threatens to hit Iran infrastructure in profanity-laden social media post
stories.summary: A US military pilot has been found in Iran after a search-and-rescue mission. Soon after, US President Donald Trump issued a threat to Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed in a profanity-laden social media post.

stories.title: We've gone mad for puzzles. This makes sense – it’s reassuring to have answers in these perplexing times | Joseph de Weck
stories.summary: Our world feels chaotic, confusing and unfair, but puzzles offer clear rules, solvable problems and reward for effort expended Maybe you’ve noticed it too. Everyone seems to have become fixated on puzzle games. In the morning, over coffee, I play Word Wheel on the Guardian app. Over lunch, colleagues compare notes on Tradle , the game where you guess a country from its exports. Which place exports about 45% fish and 50% crustaceans? Greenland. Another friend can’t fall asleep without her nightly

stories.title: When Suzuki met Suzuki: why a Tokyo dating agency is matching couples with the same name
stories.summary: Japan’s ban on married couples having different surnames has prompted an event to highlight people’s reluctance to change their name At the very least, the three men and three women calming their nerves on a Friday evening at a venue in Tokyo know they have one thing in common. Spaced out across booths, they will soon be placed in pairs and given 15 minutes to get to know one another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Az Artemis-2 legénységére mostantól jobban hat a holdi gravitáció, mint a földi
stories.summary: Több mint 50 év után először haladnak át emberek a Hold befolyási területén.

stories.title: Egy fóti templomban elhangzott a húsvéti misén: Gyurcsány Jézus ellensége
stories.summary: Néhány város egyházközössége is beszállt a kampányba, Kecskeméten például a helyi fideszes országgyűlési képviselőnek köszönték meg a frissen érkezett plébániafelújítási támogatást.

stories.title: Föld alatt ragadt bányászok, halálos áldozatok: Ukrajna egy luhanszki szénbányát, az oroszok Odesszát támadták az éjjel
stories.summary: Az odesszai támadásban egy gyerek is meghalt.

stories.title: Szigoríthat az EKB, ha elszállnak az energiaárak
stories.summary: Jánisz Szturnárasz, az Európai Központi Bank Kormányzótanácsának tagja szerint szigorúbb monetáris politika várható, ha tartós lesz az energiaárak növekedése.

stories.title: Lövöldözés történt a Fehér Ház környékén
stories.summary: A titkosszolgálat nyomoz az ügyben, egyelőre nem találtak gyanúsítottat.

stories.title: Lepsénynél meghibásodott egy Intercity, egy órás a késés a balatoni vonalon
stories.summary: Tényleg itt a tavasz.

stories.title: Zabolátlan művészet, pszichiátriai esetektől
stories.summary: Folyamatos gyógykezelés, pszichiátriai ellátás, lét a társadalom peremén. A Capa Központ április végéig meghosszabbított kiállításán ott a nyers, ösztönös művészet, néha totális ismeretlenektől.

stories.title: Több mint egy napon keresztül bujkált az iráni hegyekben a lelőtt amerikai F–15-ös tisztje, mire kimenekítették
stories.summary: A mentőakcióba több száz embert vontak be, a CIA elterelő műveletet is indított.

stories.title: Meghalt Takács Lajos séf
stories.summary: A legendás Olimpia étterem alapítója és szakácsa 53 éves volt.

stories.title: Az ukrán pénzszállítók ügyvédje szerint ha Lázár János igazat mond, terrorcselekményt hajtott végre a kormány
stories.summary: Laczó Adrienn szerint ha valaki jelentős anyagi javakat kerít hatalmába, és másik államhoz vagy állami szervezethez intézett követeléstől teszi függővé annak a szabadon engedését, az a terrorcselekmény egyik elkövetési magatartása.

stories.title: Újra hírt olvas a műsorvezető az NBC-ben, akinek elrabolhatták az anyját
stories.summary: A műsorvezetőt rajongók várták a stúdió előtt, üdvözölve visszatérését, de két hónapja eltűnt édesanyjáról még mindig semmi nyom.

stories.title: A Batthyány téren és a rakparton tartja eredményváróját a Tisza Párt
stories.summary: A Tisza a két évvel ezelőtti választási bulija is a Duna-parton volt, a Fideszé pedig a szemközti oldalon, a Bálnában.

stories.title: A flandriai kerékpárversenyen győztes Tadej Pogačart is megbüntethetik, ha átment a vasúti átjáró piros lámpáján
stories.summary: Az ügyészség nemcsak őt, hanem a harmadik Remco Evenepoelt is vizsgálja.

stories.title: Mit olvassanak azok a gyerekek, akiket a legnehezebb rávenni az olvasásra?
stories.summary: Az olvasóvá nevelésnek nincsenek biztos eredménnyel kecsegtető módszerei, de kár lenne teljesen feladni a kísérletezést. Ebben segít a Karakter könyves gyűjtőcikke, amelyet ifjú krimirajongóknak, történészeknek, állatbarátoknak, varázslóknak, ambiciózus lányoknak és szüleiknek ajánlunk.

stories.title: „Videózhatsz, fogkefereklám” – mondta Pócs János egy vezetéket nézegető jászapáti tiszás háziorvosnak
stories.summary: Farády Zoltán orvos saját bevallása szerint egy csővezetéket ellenőrzött, amin keresztül a gyanú szerint termálvizet lopnak, Pócs János szerint tiszások veszélyeztették a helyi ipari park energiaellátását.

stories.title: Egykori szocialista nagyágyúk nyílt levélben szólítják visszalépésre az állva maradt baloldali jelölteket
stories.summary: „Az utolsó percekben vagyunk. Lépjetek vissza!” – írta többek között Lamperth Mónika, Lendvai Ildikó, Göncz Kinga és Bárándy Péter.

stories.title: Hősként ünneplik a 14 éves bosnyák labdaszedőt, aki kiszedte Donnarumma törölközője alól a súgócetlit
stories.summary: Az olasz kapus a negyedik, utolsó kísérletnél találta el először a helyes irányt a keddi pótselejtezőben.

stories.title: Az Artemis–2 űrhajósai rekordot döntöttek, náluk messzebb még sosem járt ember az űrben
stories.summary: Előre lehetett tudni, hogy rekordot dönt majd a küldetés, ez most már hivatalosan is megtörtént.

stories.title: Dozens missing after migrant boat capsizes in Mediterranean
stories.summary: A boat departed from Libya carrying more than 100 people. Only 32 people are confirmed to have survived its voyage in the Mediterranean Sea.

stories.title: Nigeria's army rescues 31 hostages after church attack
stories.summary: The army ​said the attack had occurred ‌during Easter celebrations at a church in northwestern Kaduna state. Northwest Nigeria has struggled with violence for years.

stories.title: Displaced in Lebanon: 'Lives turned upside down'
stories.summary: Over 1.1 million Lebanese have fled their homes due to fighting in the south of the country and Israeli bombing of Beirut's suburbs. DW met a family struggling to preserve a sense of normality in tough conditions.

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran reportedly rejects ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Iran has given ten conditions as it seeks a permanent end to the war, IRNA news agency reported. Meanwhile, Israel has targeted a key petrochemicals site. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Russians living in exile cope with grief far from home
stories.summary: Since the start of the war against Ukraine, many Russians living in exile have been unable to return home for security reasons. Three of them told DW how they cope with the loss of loved ones far from their families.

stories.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
stories.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

stories.title: Iran war suspends Easter church gatherings in Dubai, but Christians remain defiant against attacks
stories.summary: Due to safety concerns as Iran’s attacks continue, churches in Dubai suspended Easter in-person masses to avoid large gatherings. Catholic Christians in the UAE and the Gulf have embraced Easter’s message with hope and defiance amid the constant threat.

stories.title: Artemis's stunning Moon pictures - science or holiday photos?
stories.summary: The story behind the beautiful pictures beamed back to Earth from the Artemis II astronauts.

stories.title: In first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV urges those who can unleash wars to 'choose peace'
stories.summary: In his first Easter blessing, Pope Leo XIV urged "those who have the power to unleash wars" to "choose peace" as Christians around the world celebrate Easter Sunday.

stories.title: More than 70 people feared dead after migrant boat capsizes in the central Mediterranean
stories.summary: A boat carrying around 105 people capsized in the central Mediterranean Sea after departing from Libya on Saturday. Two bodies were recovered and at least 70 are missing, according to NGOs Mediterranea Saving Humans and Sea-Watch.

stories.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The BBC's Orla Guerin travels to the edge of the critical waterway that Iran has put a stranglehold on.

stories.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to cope with child's autism in a country with little help
stories.summary: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.

stories.title: US Secret Service investigates reports of gunfire near White House
stories.summary: Officials said "a heightened security posture is in place", but no injuries were reported and no suspects had been identified.

stories.title: Researchers unveil user-friendly robotic arm in Mexico
stories.summary: In Guadalajara, Mexico, researchers created the E-Redi prosthetic arm, a simpler bionic limb using one muscle sensor to control multiple natural movements.

stories.title: Biometric checks stalled again for cross-Channel travellers
stories.summary: Fears of Easter chaos over scaling up of new EU border system are eased, with no facial IDs for Eurotunnel and Eurostar passengers Passengers crossing the Channel from the UK to France will not face new biometric checks in the coming weeks, despite an imminent deadline for the complete implementation of the EU’s entry-exit system (EES), ports say. Airlines and airports across Europe have feared chaos over the Easter holidays. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fifteen dead in 'massive' Russian attack while Kyiv signals readiness for Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed 15 people in a "massive" assault on Ukraine on Friday and into Saturday while Kyiv proposed an Easter truce and launched retaliatory drone strikes deep into Russian territory.

stories.title: US and Iran race to find missing downed pilot as Lebanon faces intense Israeli strikes
stories.summary: The Israeli military have relaunched evacuation orders from the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, warning of more strikes, claiming they are targeting Hezbollah. Over 1.2 million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the beginning of the war.

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Germany news: Baltic humpback whale 'Timmy' still breathing
stories.summary: The plight of a whale marooned in shallow Baltic waters has had animal lovers gripped for the past week. Meanwhile, Germany's Easter peace marches continue as the Iran war rages.

stories.title: Young German men refusing military service
stories.summary: Military service remains voluntary in Germany. However, with the Bundeswehr needing more soldiers, conscription could soon return. But an increasing number of young men are choosing to opt out.

stories.title: German men need military permit for extended stays abroad
stories.summary: The new military service law requires all men under 45 to seek approval from the Bundeswehr to leave the country for longer than three months. It also obliges the military career center to issue it.

stories.title: Germany's new religious diversity
stories.summary: In Germany, many Christian churches are abandoned. In their place, other religious buildings are emerging. Immigrants from India or Syria bring their matters of faith, making the urban landscape increasingly diverse.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV bares cross for full Good Friday procession, a feat not seen in decades
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV marked his first Good Friday by becoming the first pontiff in decades to personally carry the wooden cross for all 14 stations at the Colosseum.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV carries cross through all 14 stations at Rome’s Colosseum
stories.summary: Italy witnessed a rare and highly symbolic Good Friday ceremony on 3 April as Pope Leo XIV carried a wooden cross through all 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at Rome’s Colosseum, the first time in decades that a pope has performed the full rite in person.

stories.title: Middle East tensions drives global food prices higher for second month
stories.summary: The UN reported a second monthly rise in global food prices as the Middle East conflict drives up energy and fertilizer costs, threatening future harvests and yields despite currently stable market supplies.

stories.title: What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces are searching for a missing American crew member after a US warplane was shot down.

stories.title: New German conscription law: eligible men may now need approval for trips abroad
stories.summary: Men aged 17 to 45 may now need approval from the Bundeswehr for trips abroad lasting more than three months. What is behind the new military service law?

stories.title: Prayers, verses and holy books: How have religious symbols been used in the ongoing war in the Middle East?
stories.summary: The messages exchanged in the Middle East reflect a different face of warfare. Religious rhetoric is being employed in an uncommon and increasing way, with symbols of the three major monotheistic religions overlapping: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam in the middle of war.

stories.title: 'Absolutely spectacular': Artemis II crew see first glimpse of far side of Moon
stories.summary: Astronauts on the Artemis II mission are on the third day of their journey around the far side of the Moon.

stories.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
stories.summary: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuter

stories.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing appointed president after ‘sham’ election
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing seized control five years ago and plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos Min Aung Hlaing, the military general who plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos when he took power in the 2021 coup has been appointed president, months after widely condemned sham elections. Min Aung Hlaing, who is wanted by the prosecutor of the international criminal court for crimes against humanity against the Rohingya Muslim minority, was voted president by lawmakers on Friday. Mya

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Pakistan and China propose five-part peace plan for Middle East
stories.summary: Foreign ministers Ishaq Dar and Wang Yi met in Beijing as Pakistan pushes for peacemaker role Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan and China have released a joint five-part proposal for peace in the Middle East, after Pakistan’s foreign minister flew to Beijing on Tuesday to seek Chinese support for the country’s faltering efforts to negotiate an end to end the war. The one-day meeting between Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, came as Pakistan continues to push for the role o

stories.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
stories.summary: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’ People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘India is going to face a food crisis’: Farmers panic over fertiliser shortages amid Iran war
stories.summary: Ripple effects of oil and fertiliser shortage felt by farmers in India and Sri Lanka despite governments saying there is enough stock to go round Gurvinder Singh never thought the war in Iran would touch his quiet corner of Punjab. Yet looking out over his smallholding, where he alternates between wheat and rice crops in the state known as India’s breadbasket, the 52-year-old farmer can barely think of anything else. His anxiety over a conflict playing out thousands of miles away is crippling as

stories.title: Serbian local elections: Is Vucic's party losing its grip?
stories.summary: Serbia's ruling SNS once again secured victories in local elections — but sometimes just by a few hundred votes. Narrowing margins suggest a shifting political landscape and growing momentum for anti-government protests.

stories.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills at least 29
stories.summary: The plane crashed in a mountainous forest area not far from Crimea's capital, Simferopol. Since the Kremlin sent troops into Ukraine, incidents involving Russian military planes have occurred frequently.

stories.title: US launches rescue operation after state TV says American fighter jet downed in Iran
stories.summary: The incident is the first report of a US fighter jet being downed in Iranian territory since the United States and Israel launched its war on the Islamic republic on 28 February.

stories.title: Iran reeling after B1 bridge strike as Trump threatens further attacks
stories.summary: Images taken during a press tour of the site show collapsed sections of the roadway, twisted steel girders and extensive damage in the surrounding residential area where the B1 bridge near Karaj once stood.

stories.title: Ukraine’s Zhytomyr region reels after missile and drone strike
stories.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes hit Ukraine’s Zhytomyr region, killing one and injuring 10, as dozens of homes and public buildings were destroyed.

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the cost of living in Britain is to push for de-escalation in the Middle East, and a reopening o

stories.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warning One person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves. The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Co

stories.title: Crete skies turn eerie orange after Sahara dust storm – video
stories.summary: A Saharan dust storm blanketed the Mediterranean island of Crete as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from north Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted. The dust storm came as heavy rain and gale-force winds battered Greece, flooding dozens of homes and leading to at least one death Continue reading...

stories.title: Orange skies over Crete as Storm Erminio sweeps Saharan sand across Greek island – in pictures
stories.summary: Storm Erminio has caused widespread destruction in parts of Greece, including the Attica region where a man died in Nea Makri. On the island of Crete, skies turned an eerie orange as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from North Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted Continue reading...

stories.title: A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.title: Storm Erminio lashes much of Greece as Saharan dust blankets Crete
stories.summary: South-east Italy also affected by heavy rain, and snow at higher altitudes, while deadly flooding hits Afghanistan Parts of the Mediterranean have been lashed by Storm Erminio this week. Heavy rain, thunderstorms and occasional bursts of hail affected much of Greece throughout Wednesday and Thursday, with the most severe conditions across south-eastern parts of the mainland and several islands in the southern Aegean Sea, including Crete, with streets flooded and vehicles stranded. Some of the he

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, a Russian drone attack in Kharkiv, a Saharan dust storm in Crete and the launch of Artemis II – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Architect of the Easter Rising, hanged as a traitor: for Roger Casement, a pardon still seems far away | Rory Carroll
stories.summary: Relations with Britain have improved again since Brexit, but battles over Irish history remain visible in Stormont’s endless feuding Rory Carroll is the Guardian’s Ireland correspondent and author of A Rebel and a Traitor: A Fugitive, the Manhunt and Birth of the IRA More than a century after he was marched to the gallows, there is still something radioactive about Roger Casement , a name that continues to emit a faint crackle in British-Irish relations. He was knighted in 1911 by King George V

stories.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
stories.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

stories.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
stories.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.
